segments.integrated_caption: The scene opens with a wide shot of the men's high jump final at the World Athletics Championships Oregon 22 in Eugene, Oregon, USA, showcasing the high jump bar, colorful landing mat, and a packed stadium under a clear blue sky, as an announcer welcomes a global audience to this dedicated coverage. The camera then focuses on Mutaz Essa Barshim, the Qatari athlete known as 'the prince,' wearing a red jacket with 'QATAR' on the back and a white cap, standing near the track, appearing focused and composed. An on-screen graphic displays the starting lineup for the men's high jump final, listing 13 athletes with their countries, names, and world rankings, including top contenders like Sanghyeok WOO (KOR, world rank 2), mentioned by the announcer as 'Woosang Yuck' and one of the favorites, and Giannmarco Tamberi (ITA, world rank 1). The announcer details the jumping order, confirming Barshim in fourth position and Tamberi in eighth. The scene transitions to show Barshim interacting casually with Giannmarco Tamberi, the Italian athlete in a blue shirt with an Italian flag patch, who adjusts his hair with a relaxed demeanor. The announcer continues to introduce the 13 athletes lining up on the apron to compete for gold. The final shot features Edgar Rivera, the Mexican jumper, wearing a red jacket and black pants, standing confidently on the track with his hands behind his back, identified by on-screen text as world rank 20, age 31, with a personal best of 2.31m and season best of 2.27m, as the announcer identifies him as the first up in the order. The vibrant and competitive atmosphere of the stadium, with bright sunlight and a large engaged audience, is palpable throughout.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens in a brightly lit stadium with packed stands and 'OREGON 22' banners, as an announcer introduces the 'MEN'S HIGH JUMP | FINAL' finalists. Edgar RIVERA, the Mexican athlete in a red jacket and black pants, stands confidently with hands behind his back, identified by on-screen text as world rank 20, age 31, with a personal best of 2.31m and season best of 2.27m. A commentator notes he is the 'Mexican national record holder' who 'came very close to having a World Championship medal,' placing 'fourth in London,' and will 'jump fifth in the order.' The camera then pans to Joel BADEN of Australia, wearing a green and yellow sleeveless top, smiling as he walks forward. His stats are displayed (world rank 25, age 26, personal best 2.30m, season best 2.28m), and the commentator adds this is 'his third Worlds, looking for his first final,' and he'll 'go 11th.' Next, Luis Enrique ZAYAS of Cuba appears in a blue and red tracksuit with a white cap, making a peace sign. His stats show world rank 37, age 25, personal best 2.33m, season best 2.30m, while the commentator highlights he has 'the hardest national record in the world to break' and was 'fifth in Doha,' possessing significant experience. Yonathan KAPITOLNIK of Israel, in a blue shirt, walks calmly, with stats showing world rank 23, age 19, personal best 2.30m, season best 2.30m. The commentator identifies him as the 'World Under 20 gold medalist just last year,' now 'jumping in a senior championship.' Mateusz PRZYBYLKO of Germany, in a yellow long-sleeve top with the German eagle, gestures near his face. His stats are world rank 32, age 30, personal best 2.35m, season best 2.30m, and he's an '11-time national champ' from a country with a 'great deep rich history of high jumpers.' Finally, Tomohiro SHINNO of Japan, in a red-orange shirt with 'JAPAN' and the Asics logo, walks forward with a composed expression, his stats showing world rank 17, age 25, personal best 2.31m, season best 2.30m, as the commentator begins to detail his achievements, mentioning 'twice.' Throughout, the consistent stadium environment with a red track and a large landing mat is visible, accompanied by the general buzz of a live event and commentator speech.

segments.integrated_caption: The men's high jump final at the World Athletics Championships Oregon 22 commences under bright sunlight in a packed stadium, with athletes confidently walking across the red track. On-screen graphics display their details as they approach the high jump area. First, Canadian athlete Django Lovett, in a red 'CANADA' jersey and black shorts, walks with a composed expression; the announcer identifies him as the Canadian national champion who set a season best of 2.30m last month, will jump second, and secured his first Diamond League win in Birmingham earlier this year. He is followed by Japanese athlete Tomohiro Shinno, in a red-orange 'JAPAN' shirt, who raises his arm in a gesture of readiness. Next, Ukrainian athlete Andriy Protsenko, in a bright yellow jacket and blue pants, raises both arms in a celebratory motion, with the announcer highlighting his special motivation for his seventh World Championship, noting it's his first time jumping on the second day and in the final. American athlete Shelby McEwen, in a navy blue tracksuit, adjusts his collar with a focused look; the announcer introduces him as the US National Champion with the highest mark in the field at 2.33m, slated to be the 12th jumper. Another American, JuVaughn Harrison, in a similar navy blue outfit, turns and raises his arms dynamically before revealing a USA flag draped over his shoulders, eliciting a 'big roar' from the crowd. The announcer recalls his wins in both collegiate high jump and long jump at Hayward Field a year prior, a feat he repeated at the National Championships. South Korean athlete Sanghyeok Woo, in a white long-sleeve shirt, gestures with his hand; the announcer identifies him as the indoor World Champion from Korea, who placed fourth in Tokyo with a national record. Finally, Italian athlete Gianmarco Tamberi, in a blue shirt, walks forward while adjusting his hair. Banners reading 'OREGON 22' and 'World Meet' are visible, and the high jump apparatus stands in the background, all contributing to the lively, competitive atmosphere.

segments.integrated_caption: The post-competition scene at the Oregon 22 World Athletics Championships captures Mutaz Essa BARSHIM, the Qatari high jump athlete, walking away from the high jump area with a composed demeanor. As he moves along the red track, passing officials and photographers, a commentator remarks, "That'll do it," and anticipates him taking his medal and flowers, noting "bouquets from the crowd." Barshim approaches his belongings, and the commentator declares, "Barshim again, he'll stamp himself... He's one of the greatest all time." He bends down to pick up a red jacket, then puts on a red QATAR-branded shirt over his singlet, adjusting his cap and gold-tinted sunglasses. A distinct voice exclaims, "I am high jump!" which the commentator affirms, "Yes he is. I am high jump." Barshim then retrieves the Qatari national flag, unfurls it with both arms raised in celebration, and walks proudly across the track, draped in the flag. The stadium is filled with spectators under bright sunlight, with 'OREGON 22' banners visible, as the commentator highlights Barshim's historic achievement: "Champion in 2017, champion in 2019. The only man ever to win back-to-back World Championships in the high jump, he's now gone back-to-back to back," all while the crowd's celebratory buzz fills the air.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with a wide-angle view of the packed stadium under bright sunlight and a clear blue sky during the Men's High Jump Final at the World Athletics Championships Oregon 22. An on-screen graphic displays the final results, showing Mutaz Essa BARSHIM (QAT) in first place with 2.37m, Sanghyeok WOO (KOR) at 2.35m, and Andriy PROTSENKO (UKR) and Gianmarco TAMBERI (ITA) both at 2.33m. A commentator's voice confirms these standings, stating, "The gold medalist was 31, the silver medalist 26, the bronze medalist is Protsenko." He then points out, "There they are. Barshim, Woo, Protsenko," and adds that "Tamberi helped give the fans a show with his clearances, with his personality, with his charisma." The commentator also acknowledges "Shelby McEwen in a fine championship as he went through," and mentions "a lot of guys at 227" as the scoreboard updates to include positions 11 to 13, showing Yonathan KAPITOLNIK (ISR) and Mateusz PRZYBYLKO (GER) tied at 2.24m. He specifically notes, "Edgar Rivera, Mexico, 219, made the opening height." The graphic remains static, emphasizing the final standings, as the commentator concludes, "the championship belongs to Barshim." The scene then transitions to a black screen featuring the colorful 'World Athletics Championships Oregon 22' logo and social media handles, followed by the hashtag '#WORLDATHLETICSCHAMPS' against abstract shapes, marking the event's celebratory and conclusive end.
